description_long: This is a large, multi-tiered pagoda structure with seven distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging eaves. The roofs are gently sloped and feature upward-curving eaves, a hallmark of traditional East Asian architecture. The exterior is primarily composed of gray and beige materials, with wooden railings and support beams visible on each level. The base is broad and square, providing a stable foundation for the tall, tapering structure. At the apex, there is a small, decorative finial that crowns the roof. The overall design is symmetrical and highly ornamental, evoking the grandeur of historic Chinese pagodas.
description: A tall, seven-story pagoda with upward-curving eaves, gray roofs, and a decorative finial, built in a traditional East Asian architectural style.
description_view_0: The front view shows the symmetrical facade, with all seven stories and the central entrance clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view is nearly identical to the front, highlighting the layered roofs and central axis of the pagoda.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with the same tiered structure and visible balconies on each level.
description_view_3: This side view is similar to the others, emphasizing the depth and width of the base and the stacked roof design.
